english: 1. the letter k, tenuis, = French c in car. — 2. as numerical figure, used in marking the volumes of a work: one. — ka-t'o (ka tho) alphabetical register Sch. — ká-pa (ka pa) the first volume of a work. — ka-dpé (ka dpe) a-b-c-book. — ka-p'réṅ (ka phreng), ka smad sum-ču (ka smad sum cu), kā-li (kA li) the Tibetan alphabet.
1. an additional syllable, so-called article, affixed to some substantives, numerals and pronouns, v. the grammars. — 2. pillar, v. ka-ba (ka ba).
oh!
excrement, (nursery word), *ka-ka taṅ-če* (*ka ka tang ce*) W. = French: faire caca.
Ssk. crow.
Ssk. कङ्क, heron.
a small coin of ancient India Cs.
cucumber Kun.
Ssk. कक्कोल, a plant bearing a berry the inner part of which is a waxlike and aromatic substance. — ka-ko (ka ko) prob. means the same.
the a-b-c, alphabet; ka-k'ai t'o (ka kha'i tho) alphabetical register, ka-k'ai dpe (ka kha'i dpe) a-b-c-book; ka-k'a-pa (ka kha pa) abecedarian.
v. k'ol-ma (khol ma).
also ká-č'a (ka cha), goods, things; ká-čai rjés-su ḥbráṅs-pai rgyálpo (ka ca'i rjes su 'brangs pa'i rgyal po) n. of a demon.
also ka-tya (ka tya). n. of a locality Mil.
v. ke-tu (ke tu).
more correctly ka-ṭo-ra (ka To ra), Hd., metal cup, dish, basin.
Kun. a sort of peach.
also ka-nas dag (ka nas dag), pure from the beginning Lex.
(from خبردار Urd.?) only in the phrase: *ka-dar čo-če* (*ka dar co ce*) to be cautious, take care, take heed, -la (-la), of.
Ssk. skull.
gum, resin Sch.
absurd spelling instead of kam-bo-ja (kam bo ja) Wdk.

*ka-wa* (*ka wa*) 1. pillar, post; k. dzug-pa (k. dzug pa) to erect a pillar. — 2. a large vein or artery in the abdomen. — Comp. ka-skéd (ka sked) shaft of a column. — ká čan (ka can) having columns. — ka-gčig-sgo-gčig (ka gcig sgo gcig) a small house, poor cottage; also a mode of capital punishment is said to be called so, when the culprit is fastened to a pillar in a dungeon until he dies of hunger. — ka-gčig-pa (ka gcig pa) having one pillar, ka-máṅ-ma (ka mang ma) having many pillars. — ka-čéṅ (ka chen) the principal p. (cf. στύλος Gal. 2. 9) Tar. 182. 10. — ka-rtén (ka rten) base of a p. Lex. — ka-stégs (ka stegs), ka-gdán (ka gdan) pedestal, base of a p. — ka-spuṅs (ka spungs) many pillars. — ka-méd (ka med) without a pillar; helpless, destitute. — ka-rtsé (ka rtse), ka-yáṅ-rtse (ka yang rtse) capital of a pillar. — ka wá- (ka wa)
